root_cause: format("delta").load(path) reads Delta Lake tables by path. Not supported in Snowpark Connect — Snowflake does not use Delta Lake format natively. Replace with session.table("my_table") for Snowflake-managed tables. For external Delta data: 1) Create an external table over Delta files using CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ... WITH LOCATION = ... FILE_FORMAT = (TYPE = DELTA), or 2) Use Iceberg tables (preferred). Time travel: use Snowflake's AT(TIMESTAMP => ...) or AT(OFFSET => -N) instead of versionAsOf.

format("delta").save(path) writes data in Delta Lake format. Not supported in Snowpark Connect. Replace with: df.write.save_as_table("output_table", mode="overwrite") for Snowflake-managed tables. partitionBy() is not needed — Snowflake handles micro-partitioning automatically. For external output, write to a Snowflake stage in Parquet format.

code:
from delta.tables import DeltaTable
deltaTable = DeltaTable.forPath(spark, "/mnt/data/events")
deltaTable = DeltaTable.forName(spark, "my_catalog.my_schema.events")
root_cause: DeltaTable.forPath()/forName() creates a DeltaTable reference for DML operations. Not available in Snowpark Connect — the delta.tables module does not exist. Replace with session.table("events") for read operations. For DML (merge, update, delete), use session.sql() with Snowflake SQL syntax.

code:
from delta.tables import DeltaTable
deltaTable = DeltaTable.forPath(spark, path)
deltaTable.alias("target").merge(
    source_df.alias("source"),
    "target.id = source.id"
).whenMatchedUpdateAll().whenNotMatchedInsertAll().execute()
root_cause: DeltaTable.merge() API for upsert operations. Not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake SQL MERGE: session.sql("MERGE INTO target USING source ON target.id = source.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET target.col1 = source.col1, ... WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (col1, ...) VALUES (source.col1, ...)"). Note: Snowflake does not support updateAll()/insertAll() shorthand — list columns explicitly.

DeltaTable conditional merge with whenNotMatchedBySource. Snowflake MERGE supports WHEN MATCHED AND condition THEN UPDATE SET ... and WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ... Use session.sql() with full MERGE syntax. Note: WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE is supported in Snowflake (DELETE or UPDATE the target rows with no match in source).

DeltaTable.update() for conditional updates. Replace with Snowflake SQL: session.sql("UPDATE my_table SET status = 'processed', processed_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() WHERE status = 'pending'"). Or use Snowpark DataFrame: session.table("my_table").update({"status": lit("processed")}, col("status") == "pending").

DeltaTable.delete() for conditional/full deletes. Replace with Snowflake SQL: session.sql("DELETE FROM my_table WHERE date < '2023-01-01'"). Or use Snowpark DataFrame: session.table("my_table").delete(col("date") < "2023-01-01"). Full delete: session.sql("TRUNCATE TABLE my_table").

code:
spark.sql("OPTIMIZE delta.`/mnt/data/events`")
spark.sql("OPTIMIZE my_table ZORDER BY (date, category)")
root_cause: Delta Lake OPTIMIZE command compacts small files and applies Z-ordering. Not needed in Snowpark Connect — Snowflake handles micro-partition compaction automatically. For query optimization, use CLUSTER BY on the table: ALTER TABLE my_table CLUSTER BY (date, category). Snowflake's automatic clustering replaces both OPTIMIZE and ZORDER.

code:
spark.sql("VACUUM delta.`/mnt/data/events` RETAIN 168 HOURS")
spark.sql("VACUUM my_table")
root_cause: Delta Lake VACUUM removes old files beyond retention period. Not applicable in Snowpark Connect — Snowflake manages storage automatically via Time Travel retention. Configure retention with: ALTER TABLE my_table SET DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS = 7. Snowflake automatically reclaims storage after the retention period.

DeltaTable.history() shows the transaction log / version history. Replace with Snowflake's query history and Time Travel: session.sql("SELECT * FROM my_table AT(TIMESTAMP => '2024-01-01'::TIMESTAMP)") for point-in-time access. For change tracking, use Snowflake Streams: CREATE STREAM my_stream ON TABLE my_table.

Delta Lake time travel via versionAsOf/timestampAsOf options. Replace with Snowflake Time Travel: session.sql("SELECT * FROM my_table AT(OFFSET => -3)") for version-based, session.sql("SELECT * FROM my_table AT(TIMESTAMP => '2024-01-01 00:00:00'::TIMESTAMP_LTZ)") for timestamp-based. Max retention: 90 days (Enterprise edition).

Delta Lake schema evolution via mergeSchema option. Snowflake handles schema evolution differently: 1) For Snowflake-managed tables, use ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN for explicit changes, 2) For COPY INTO, set MATCH_BY_COLUMN_NAME = CASE_INSENSITIVE. Automatic schema evolution is available with Snowpipe for semi-structured data.

Delta Lake Change Data Feed (CDF) for tracking row-level changes. Replace with Snowflake Streams: CREATE STREAM my_stream ON TABLE my_table SHOW_INITIAL_ROWS = TRUE. Query changes: SELECT * FROM my_stream. Streams track INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE with METADATA$ACTION and METADATA$ISUPDATE columns.

DeltaTable.createIfNotExists() builder API. Replace with Snowflake DDL: session.sql("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (id BIGINT, data VARCHAR, date DATE)"). No partitionedBy needed — Snowflake handles micro-partitioning automatically. Optionally add clustering: CLUSTER BY (date).

DeltaTable.convertToDelta() converts Parquet/Iceberg tables to Delta format in-place. No Snowpark Connect equivalent — this is a Delta Lake file management operation. To ingest Parquet data into Snowflake: session.sql("COPY INTO my_table FROM @my_stage FILE_FORMAT = (TYPE = PARQUET)") or create an external table over existing Parquet files.
